reasoning:
The comparison price is the market close on 2026-03-01 (corresponding to Friday, February 27). Stock prices broadly follow a lognormal random walk with a slight upward drift over time. 

For the first resolution date (March 8), which is a 1-week horizon (March 6 close), the stock will exhibit standard market drift with no known dividend payouts. Considering the long-term equity premium and DLR's recent stable momentum around $176, the prospect of an increase is just slightly over a coin toss, estimated at 51%.

For the second resolution date (March 31), DLR will pass its ex-dividend date on March 13, 2026, paying out a $1.22 per share dividend. This creates a mechanical drag of approximately 0.7% on the unadjusted closing price. The expected monthly upward drift is typically less than this absolute dividend drop. Although standard deviation increases with a 1-month horizon, making wide moves in either direction more likely, the central expectation (median) is slightly below the starting price. This pushes the probability below 50%, estimated at 49%.
